inputs.problem_text:
n 2016,  27.54% of total A/R overdue all customer in ID03 with 51.8 DSO, so we will focus on this for this project. 
High A/R overdue shows that a company is selling its product to customer on credit and taking longer to collect money. It means company has less readily available cash to finance our operation. In addition, high A/R overdue will be blocked the orders and CSD cannot send the product until the payment has been made by customer
inputs.goal_text: Improve the cashflow with reduce monthly overdue A/R customer to 12.8 % and monthly DSO to 50 days by end of Nov 2017.
inputs.voc_list:
Head of Sales : "Reduce A/R overdue & DSO all customers in ID03, target overdue A/R 12,8% and 50 DSO for SEAP"
Sales & SCM : To many blocked SO caused by A/R overdue. They cannot fullfill customer request to deliver the goods.

outputs.problem_statement: In 2016, 27.54% of total A/R was overdue for all customers in ID03 with a DSO of 51.8 days. This indicates that the company is taking longer to collect payments, resulting in less readily available cash to finance operations. High A/R overdue also leads to blocked orders, preventing CSD from sending products until payments are made.
outputs.goal_statement: Improve cash flow by reducing monthly overdue A/R to 12.8% and monthly DSO to 50 days by the end of November 2017.
outputs.business_case: Reducing A/R overdue will improve cash flow, allowing the company to finance operations more effectively. This will also reduce blocked orders, enabling better customer service and potentially increasing sales.

in_scope: Entire A/R process within the Supply Chain

ctq_list.name: A/R Overdue Percentage
ctq_list.description: The percentage of accounts receivable that are overdue beyond 30 days.

ctq_list.name: Days Sales Outstanding (DSO)
ctq_list.description: The average number of days it takes to collect payment after a sale has been made.

outputs.y_variable: Percentage of A/R overdue beyond 30 days

Man: Training for billing and collections staff
Staffing levels in the A/R department
Environment: Economic conditions affecting customer payment behavior

decision_trace.focus_ctq_strategy: Identify critical-to-quality (CTQ) factors related to the Accounts Receivable (A/R) process, focusing on reducing overdue payments and improving cash flow.
decision_trace.y_variable_candidate: Percentage of A/R overdue beyond 30 days
decision_trace.scope_boundary: The scope should include the entire A/R process within the Supply Chain, from invoicing to payment collection, while excluding unrelated financial operations.
risks: Potential resistance to process changes from the billing team
Data accuracy issues in current A/R records
Possible impact on customer relationships due to stricter payment terms
decision_trace.notes_for_action: Conduct stakeholder analysis to understand the impact of overdue A/R on different departments. Engage with the billing and sales teams early to gather insights and foster collaboration. Ensure data integrity by auditing current A/R records and establishing clear metrics for overdue payments.

define_state.summary_md:
## DEFINE — project_004
**Gate Status:** FAILED

### Problem
In 2016, 27.54% of total A/R was overdue for all customers in ID03 with a DSO of 51.8 days. This indicates that the company is taking longer to collect payments, resulting in less readily available cash to finance operations. High A/R overdue also leads to blocked orders, preventing CSD from sending products until payments are made.

### Goal
Improve cash flow by reducing monthly overdue A/R to 12.8% and monthly DSO to 50 days by the end of November 2017.

---
